Web · Optimization Republic Wireless · 2018

Republic Wireless Site Optimization

Unifying the brand message and defining a clear funnel to conversion — turning a splintered shopping experience into a simple, linear path.

Save as · republic-new-homepage.webp
Redesigned homepage
Clear hierarchy, four product pillars, single CTA per card. The end-state the case study walks back from.

Redesigned homepage — clear hierarchy, four product pillars, and a single CTA per card

Role

Lead UX Strategist

Platform

Marketing website (Republic Wireless)

Collaborators

Marketing · Development team

Status

Shipped

Research

Mapping user journeys

Storyframing played a critical role in this user journey research — a great way to apply target segments directly to our designs. By designing for specific flows, we could ensure a smooth purchase process from first visit to checkout.

Because buying a phone takes time, it was important to include ways for shoppers to return. We explored saving items in the cart and showing view history for returning visitors.

Placeholder · Analytics screenshot
Google Analytics behavior flow
Save as · republic-ga-behavior-flow.png
Google Analytics behavior flow
Sankey diagram annotated with the loops between Plans, Phones, and Coverage that the new IA was designed to eliminate.

Google Analytics behavior flow showing users circling through the phones page rather than moving toward checkout

Information Architecture

New information architecture

The goal was to mirror the natural shopping journey — educate first, then purchase. We aimed to make the path clear while reducing branching in the navigation structure, and retiring marketing pages that no longer served the brand message.

Placeholder · Diagram
Revised information architecture
Save as · republic-ia-wireframes.png
IA wireframes (annotated)
Plans, Coverage, and Homepage wireframes with the design principles down the left rail — Clear & Concise, Anxiety reducers, Personalized data, Relevant data points.
Save as · republic-nav-spec.png
Top-nav design spec
Active state, hover state, and default state defined with hex tokens — the simplification of the top nav from a many-link bar to three core destinations.

Revised information architecture — a clearer top nav and a single linear path through Coverage → Plans → Phones

Mobile First

Designing for mobile-first traffic

Mobile traffic had grown to outpace desktop — so it became essential to keep mobile views clean, clear, and simple. We wireframed across key pages including homepage, plans, coverage, and shop with a mobile-first lens, then scaled up to desktop.

Mobile-first wireframes for homepage, plans, and coverage pages across prospect and returning member states

User Testing

Iterating through user feedback

Throughout the process I led and coordinated extensive user testing — including in-house guerrilla usability sessions, think-aloud studies, and moderated video interviews. Multiple rounds with 20+ participants, with each iteration informing refinements to the visual design and content strategy.

User testing findings (left) and the promotional card anatomy system developed with the marketing director (right)

Final Pages

Shop, Plans, and Coverage

To keep navigation simple, we created a dedicated Shop page as a central hub for browsing products, promoting sales, and highlighting new devices. The Plans page drove awareness of the new My Choice monthly plans, positioning Republic as the best value in wireless. The Coverage page integrated an interactive map with a passive IP-based location check to build confidence.

New shop page — a clean hub for phones, SIM kits, and promotions

Plans page (left) and coverage page with integrated map (right)

Results

A measurable shift in behavior

1.7% → 17.7%Homepage → Plans → Coverage → Phones

The redesign successfully guided users through the intended page flow. This improvement held across first-time visitors, prospects, and logged-in members — demonstrating the impact of the simplified top navigation.

Post-launch analytics showing the increase in users following the intended funnel flow

While attributing direct conversions was challenging, the redesign forced a broader review of our site strategy — aligning messaging, communications, and the shopping journey. It set the stage for faster, smarter decisions through A/B testing, content optimization, and iterative branding work.
